title: Basic
summary: Defines the critical input/output density weights, abstract GJMS operators, and operator families of order 2k.

mathematicalContent.heading: Mathematical role
mathematicalContent.body: Defines the critical input/output density weights, abstract GJMS operators, and operator families of order 2k.

mathematicalContent.heading: Formal boundary
mathematicalContent.body: This page describes exactly the declarations present in GJMSoperators/Basic.lean; claims not represented by a declaration are not counted as verified.

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Definition: input Weight. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
Definition: output Weight. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
Formal proposition: output Weight eq input Weight sub two mul. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
correspondence.leanType: theorem outputWeight_eq_inputWeight_sub_two_mul (n : ℝ) (k : ℕ) : outputWeight n k = inputWeight n k - 2 * (k : ℝ)

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Formal proposition: input Weight zero. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
correspondence.leanType: theorem inputWeight_zero (n : ℝ) : inputWeight n 0 = - n / 2

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Formal proposition: output Weight zero. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
correspondence.leanType: theorem outputWeight_zero (n : ℝ) : outputWeight n 0 = - n / 2

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Formal proposition: input Weight one. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
correspondence.leanType: theorem inputWeight_one (n : ℝ) : inputWeight n 1 = 1 - n / 2

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Formal proposition: output Weight one. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
correspondence.leanType: theorem outputWeight_one (n : ℝ) : outputWeight n 1 = -1 - n / 2

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Formal proposition: input Weight two. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
correspondence.leanType: theorem inputWeight_two (n : ℝ) : inputWeight n 2 = 2 - n / 2

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Formal proposition: output Weight two. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
correspondence.leanType: theorem outputWeight_two (n : ℝ) : outputWeight n 2 = -2 - n / 2

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Definition: calculus Input Weight. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
Definition: calculus Output Weight. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
Formal proposition: calculus Output Weight eq calculus Input Weight sub two mul. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
correspondence.leanType: theorem calculusOutputWeight_eq_calculusInputWeight_sub_two_mul (k : ℕ) : calculusOutputWeight CalConf k = calculusInputWeight CalConf k - 2 * (k : ℝ)

limitations: The displayed Lean results are checked relative to structures, typeclasses, certificates, or model laws supplied as hypotheses. The module does not by itself derive every interface field from a concrete geometric backend.
